title: Hey Baby
description: An image-centered relational call: the title addresses the viewer while the post body is a photograph of the author partly hidden behind a hand-drawn creature-like figure.

motifMap.meaning: The intimate title frames the image as a direct relational gesture rather than a captioned explanation.
A hand-drawn creature-like figure held before the face turns self-presentation into playful concealment and address.
The ordinary vehicle setting keeps the artifact grounded in a casual, immediate moment rather than a staged portrait.

associatedMedia.description: Primary Substack image post: a photograph inside a car, with the author partly hidden behind a sheet of paper showing a hand-drawn creature-like figure.
associatedMedia.altText: A person in a car holds up a white sheet of paper with a hand-drawn creature-like figure, partly covering their face.

editHistory.notes: Manual live RSS extraction performed with user permission. Classified from the live source packet as The Child of the Call with Relational subcurrent because the post has an empty body and its only textual content is a direct intimate address in the title.

Corrected after user-provided screenshots showed the post is image-centered, not title-only. RSS feed contains an image enclosure and gallery payload; metadata now records the primary image in associatedMedia while preserving the empty text body fingerprint.

integrity.notes: Filename: 2026-07-11.hey-baby.phase-the-child-of-the-call.subcurrent-relational.metadata.v2.6.5.json. Body fingerprint validates against work/live-run/hey-baby.source.txt; text body is empty because the Substack artifact is image-centered, with the primary image recorded in associatedMedia from the RSS enclosure/gallery payload.
